summary: In this brief link post, Joel Spolsky endorses Eric Sink's argument that small independent software vendors should focus on hiring solid professionals rather than chasing 'great hackers.' He also recommends Johanna Rothman's upcoming book on hiring technical talent, noting it covers an underserved topic. The post reflects Joel's pragmatic approach to building software teams.
key_points: Small ISVs are better served by hiring reliable professionals than by seeking out legendary 'great hackers'
The topic of hiring technical talent is underserved in industry literature
Joel endorses practical, grounded approaches to team building over hero-worship of elite programmers
money_quotes: It's okay to be in awe of these great hackers. But as a practical matter, small ISVs would be much better off hiring professionals.
key_insight: Small software companies should prioritize hiring dependable professionals over chasing rare 'great hackers.'
